svg.title: DDD HTTP request lifecycle diagram
svg.desc: Data flow of an HTTP POST /users request through API, Application, Domain, and Infrastructure layers, showing object transformations at each boundary

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the HTTP client send in a POST /users request?')
sendPrompt('What fields does UserCreateRequest contain and how does Pydantic validate it?')
sendPrompt('What does the FastAPI route handler do after receiving a validated request?')
sendPrompt('What is the responsibility of UserService in the application layer?')
sendPrompt('How does UserService extract primitives and call domain factories?')
sendPrompt('How do Email and Password value objects enforce domain rules?')
sendPrompt('What invariants does the User entity enforce in the domain layer?')
sendPrompt('What is the UserRepository port and why is it an interface in the domain layer?')
sendPrompt('How does SQLAlchemyUserRepository map a User entity to a UserSQL model?')
sendPrompt('What is UserSQL and why is it kept out of the domain layer?')
sendPrompt('How is UserSQL mapped back to a UserCreateResponse Pydantic schema?')
sendPrompt('How does FastAPI serialize the Pydantic response model to JSON?')

svg.text: UserCreateRequest → Email + Password (VOs) → User (entity) → UserSQL (ORM) → UserCreate (response)
each boundary enforces its own rules — Pydantic, domain invariants, ORM mapping
